response.justification: The candidate does not meet critical minimum qualifications for this role. Professional C++ experience is approximately 6 months (at National Instruments), far short of the required 8 years. Career trajectory is primarily full-stack web development (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js) rather than systems programming or infrastructure engineering. While demonstrating solid software craftsmanship and recent staff-level promotion, there is insufficient depth in C++, distributed systems architecture, or cloud infrastructure to support a technical leadership position on a cloud compute platform.
strengths: Strong software engineering practices with demonstrated commitment to testing, TDD, CI/CD, and establishing development standards
Recent Staff Software Engineer role showing leadership trajectory with full-stack capabilities across multiple technology domains
Exposure to cloud tooling (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) and event-driven platform architecture through GISCollective and Optilyz work
concerns: C++ experience is ~6 months (National Instruments role only), dramatically short of the required 8-year minimum—a critical disqualifying gap
Career focused on full-stack web development rather than systems programming, low-level infrastructure, or compute platform engineering
Large-scale distributed systems experience limited to 2 years at Dunnhumby; no demonstrated cloud or DevOps technical leadership background
